description: Refactor the admin panel from a 1940-line inline-style monolith into a modular structure using shadcn/ui components, TanStack Table, and Tailwind CSS utility classes. Wire the PulseLab navy/teal palette into shadcn CSS variables so all components inherit brand colors automatically.

userStories.title: Wire PulseLab palette into shadcn CSS variables
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shadcn components to automatically use PulseLab's navy/teal brand colors so I don't have to override them manually on every component.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/styles.css, update the :root block so --primary resolves to teal #00BFA5 in oklch: oklch(0.72 0.12 181), and --ring matches it

Update the .dark block: --background to navy #0A1628 (oklch(0.14 0.03 241)), --card to #152238 (oklch(0.19 0.04 241)), --border and --muted to #1E3048 (oklch(0.24 0.05 241)), --foreground to #F0F4F8 (oklch(0.96 0.01 250)), --muted-foreground to #8A9BB0 (oklch(0.65 0.03 243))
--primary teal value is the same in both :root and .dark blocks
Existing PulseLab-specific variables (--sea-ink, --lagoon, --palm, etc.) are left untouched
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Install @tanstack/react-table and shadcn components
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TanStack Table and the shadcn UI component files present so I can build the panel tables.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: npm install @tanstack/react-table — package appears in package.json dependencies
Run: npx shadcn@latest add button badge table card tabs sheet tooltip input checkbox scroll-area separator dropdown-menu — all components appear under src/components/ui/
If the shadcn CLI prompts about overwriting styles.css, choose NO — the file is already correctly set up
No import errors — components resolve correctly via the #/components/ui alias in tsconfig.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract StatusPill, BatteryCell, and Sparkline to own files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the three custom panel primitives in their own files under src/components/panel/ so they can be imported independently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/panel/StatusPill.tsx — exact copy of the StatusPill component currently defined inline in src/routes/_authenticated/panel/index.tsx
Create src/components/panel/BatteryCell.tsx — exact copy of the BatteryCell component from the same file
Create src/components/panel/Sparkline.tsx — exact copy of the Sparkline component from the same file

Replace any inline style props that map to CSS variables with Tailwind classes (e.g. color via text-primary instead of style={{ color: '#00BFA5' }}); hardcoded SVG geometry attributes (cx, cy, d, etc.) may remain
The original definitions in panel/index.tsx are removed and replaced with imports from @/components/panel/
Panel renders identically after the extraction
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract DeviceDrawer using shadcn Sheet
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the DeviceDrawer side panel in its own file using shadcn Sheet so it is not buried inside the monolith.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/panel/DeviceDrawer.tsx
Props interface: { device: Device | null; open: boolean; onClose: () => void }
Replace the current custom drawer with shadcn Sheet, SheetContent (side='right'), SheetHeader, SheetTitle
All content preserved: live vitals section, metric values, Sparkline charts, action buttons
Action buttons use shadcn Button component
Sparkline imported from @/components/panel/Sparkline
Zero inline styles — all layout and color via Tailwind utility classes
The open/close state stays in panel/index.tsx and is passed via props
Original drawer code removed from panel/index.tsx, replaced with <DeviceDrawer> import
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create DevicesTab with TanStack Table (basic rendering + row selection)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the devices table in its own file using TanStack Table and shadcn Table components so it is independently maintainable.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/panel/DevicesTab.tsx
Props interface: { onViewDevice: (device: Device) => void }
Define a columns array using createColumnHelper<Device>() with columns for: row select checkbox, device ID, status, battery, HRV sparkline, participant, study, last sync, actions
Use useReactTable with getCoreRowModel() and enableRowSelection: true
Row selection state managed via useState<RowSelectionState>({})
Select column renders shadcn Checkbox wired to row.getToggleSelectedHandler()
Actions column renders shadcn Button variant='ghost' with Eye, RefreshCw, Trash2 icons; Eye button calls onViewDevice(row.original)
Status column renders StatusPill imported from @/components/panel/StatusPill
Battery column renders BatteryCell imported from @/components/panel/BatteryCell
HRV column renders Sparkline imported from @/components/panel/Sparkline
Table rendered using shadcn Table, TableHeader, TableBody, TableRow, TableHead, TableCell — iterating table.getHeaderGroups() and table.getRowModel().rows with flexRender
All 10 DEVICES mock records render correctly
Zero inline styles — all styling via Tailwind classes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add sorting and search filter to DevicesTab
userStories.description: As a developer, I want DevicesTab to support column sorting and a global search input so the table is functional from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: Add getSortedRowModel() and getFilteredRowModel() to the useReactTable call in DevicesTab.tsx
Add sorting state: useState<SortingState>([]) wired to onSortingChange and state.sorting
Add globalFilter state: useState<string>('') wired to onGlobalFilterChange and state.globalFilter
Sorting enabled on columns: device ID, status, battery, last sync (enableSorting: true on those column defs)
Column headers for sortable columns render sort indicator icons: ArrowUpDown when unsorted, ArrowUp when asc, ArrowDown when desc — toggled via column.getIsSorted()
Add a shadcn Input above the table for global filter, placeholder 'Buscar dispositivo...' — wired to the globalFilter state
Global filter searches across device ID, participant, and study fields
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create ParticipantsTab with TanStack Table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the participants table in its own file using TanStack Table and shadcn Table components, following the same pattern as DevicesTab.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/panel/ParticipantsTab.tsx
Define columns using createColumnHelper<Participant>() for: select checkbox, participant ID, name, age, study, device, enrollment date, status, actions
useReactTable with getCoreRowModel(), getSortedRowModel(), getFilteredRowModel(), enableRowSelection: true
Sorting enabled on: name, age, enrollment date, status columns
shadcn Input for global filter, placeholder 'Buscar participante...'
Row selection via shadcn Checkbox in select column
Status column renders StatusPill from @/components/panel/StatusPill
Actions column renders shadcn Button variant='ghost' icon buttons (Eye, Pencil, Trash2)
Rendered using shadcn Table components with flexRender
All 10 PARTICIPANTS mock records render correctly
Zero inline styles
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create StudiesTab with TanStack Table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the studies table in its own file using TanStack Table and shadcn Table components.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/panel/StudiesTab.tsx
Define columns using createColumnHelper<Study>() for: select checkbox, study ID, title, PI, device count, participant count, progress, status, actions
useReactTable with getCoreRowModel(), getSortedRowModel(), getFilteredRowModel(), enableRowSelection: true
Sorting enabled on: title, PI, status columns
shadcn Input for global filter, placeholder 'Buscar estudo...'
Row selection via shadcn Checkbox in select column
Progress column renders a plain Tailwind div: outer div bg-muted rounded-full h-2 w-24, inner div bg-primary rounded-full h-2 with width set to the study's progress percentage
Status column renders StatusPill from @/components/panel/StatusPill
Actions column renders shadcn Button variant='ghost' icon buttons (Eye, Pencil, Trash2)
Rendered using shadcn Table components with flexRender
All 4 STUDIES mock records render correctly
Zero inline styles
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Slim panel/index.tsx to orchestrator
userStories.description: As a developer, I want panel/index.tsx to be a slim layout file under 200 lines that composes all extracted components with no inline styles.
acceptanceCriteria: panel/index.tsx imports DevicesTab, ParticipantsTab, StudiesTab, DeviceDrawer from @/components/panel/
The C design tokens constant is deleted from the file
Left sidebar navigation uses Tailwind classes only — active state toggled with cn() utility, not inline style
The sub-tab bar (Dispositivos/Alertas/Log or Participantes/TCLEs etc.) uses shadcn Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent
Top header bar (page title, subtitle, action buttons like Download and Plus) uses Tailwind + shadcn Button
DeviceDrawer open/close state remains in this file, passed to DevicesTab as onViewDevice prop and to DeviceDrawer as open/onClose props
Analytics nav item visible in sidebar but renders a Card with 'Analytics — Em breve' placeholder text
File is under 200 lines
Zero occurrences of style={{ in the file
Zero occurrences of hardcoded hex color values (no #0A1628, #00BFA5, etc.)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
